---
title: "Exploring Ibis"
format: 
  gfm:
    html-math-method: webtex
---

Ibis is a datafame library instructions (code) are separated from computation (backends). For instructions, ibis is has a syntax very similar to dplyr. 
For backends, ibis has high performance dataframe libraries, multiple RDBM systems, and multiple cloud environments (spark, snowflake, big query). In 
general, ibis code is easy to write, self-optimizing, and multi-threaded.

This repo runs two benchmarks:

* Is there a major difference in compute speed of the 3 local engines?
* Can I use polars GPU functionality with ibis?
